states.name: California
states.description: California produces about 85% of US wine with diverse growing conditions from cool coastal regions to warm inland valleys.

states.name: Oregon
states.description: Oregon is renowned for exceptional Pinot Noir from the cool, wet Willamette Valley and emerging regions in southern Oregon.

states.name: Washington
states.description: Washington's Columbia Valley benefits from desert conditions with controlled irrigation, producing bold reds and crisp whites.

states.name: New York
states.description: New York's Finger Lakes region excels in cool-climate varieties, particularly Riesling, with growing success in hybrid varieties.

states.name: Virginia
states.description: Virginia's wine industry is rapidly expanding with focus on Bordeaux varieties and exceptional Viognier in the Monticello region.

states.name: Texas
states.description: Texas High Plains AVA produces distinctive wines adapted to extreme temperature variations and high altitude growing conditions.
